hikes.name: Lough Bray Loop

swim: Lough Bray Upper (keep close to the shore)

hikes.name: Slievenamon

visit: Caherconnell stone fort and Ailwee Cave

hikes.name: Cliffs of Moher Coastal Trail
hikes.difficulty_notes: easy, but long and close to the cliff edge
hikes.route_type: out-and-back or A-to-B if two cars
hikes.special_notes: Can break the trail at Doolin, Liscannor, or the visitor centre. Trail hugs cliff edge and is only partially fenced.

visit: Day trip to the Aran Islands

hikes.name: Mount Brandon
hikes.subtitle: Mount Brandon via the Pilgrim's Path and Saints' Road
hikes.special_notes: Alternative routes: via Patternoster Lakes (very hard but stunning) and via Faha Ridge (grade 2 scrambling, requires mountain leader)

hikes.name: Mount Eagle
hikes.starting_point: Google pin location (52.13172394,-10.44062250) — Mount Eagle Start Point

alternative_routes.name: Loop via Coumeenole lake
alternative_routes.description: Can hike as a loop returning down via the lake — approx 8km. Terrain is boggy underfoot but it can be done. Also an option to hike Mount Eagle from Slea Head for a different parking option.

eat: Pig and the Leaf for food to go

hikes.parking_notes: yes, but only space for two cars max

hikes.name: Carrauntoohil
hikes.special_notes: Most standard route is via Devil's Ladder. Alternative routes (require mountain leader): Brother O'Shea's Gully via the Zigzags (14km, 5-6hrs, hard); Coomloughra Horseshoe taking in MacGillycuddy's Reeks (12.7km, 6.5-7hrs, very tough)

swim: Lakes along route of Carrauntoohil (long way back to car park)
visit: Gap of Dunloe (park at Kate Kearney's)
Wishing Bridge / The Heather for food

hikes.name: Torc Waterfall and Mountain

eat: John M. Reidy's for a pint

visit: Gap of Dunloe (park at Kate Kearney's)

visit: Cruise on the Lakes of Killarney

eat: Coffee Bee Café at Future Forests Garden Centre

visit: Florence Court Gardens and Tully Mill

hikes.name: An Port to Sturrall Ridge View

visit: Caves of Maghera if the tide is right (do not swim at this beach)

swim: The Boat Strand (the road down is very narrow)

An Chúirt Gweedore for a spot to stay for a sunset or sunrise venture

hikes.subtitle: The Miners Path

hikes.name: Dunseverick to Giant's Causeway
hikes.difficulty_notes: one way; bus back available every 30 mins from Giant's Causeway visitor entrance for £2.50 cash
hikes.route_type: A-to-B (option to hike back along cliffs or get a bus back to car park)

eat: Bothy White Park Bay Café, near Dunseverick
Two Twenty café, en route back to Belfast, very close to the Causeway
visit: Dunseverick Castle at the start of the hike
Magheracross Car Park & Viewpoint is a must to get great cliff views of the wishing arch
White Rocks Beach in Portrush and the National Trust Carrick-a-Rede tour (pre book on their site)
